title: Phase 6 Review Request
description: Written by the worker to .sc4sap/program/{PROG}/review-request.json before launching review-checklist.md in a fresh context. Tells the read-only reviewer which spec version, which system, which transport, and which objects to review.

spec_sha256.description: SHA-256 (lowercase hex) of the approved spec.md. Must equal approval.json.spec_sha256.

sid.description: SAP system ID the objects were created on.

client.description: SAP client the objects were created on.

transport.description: Transport request the created objects are recorded on.

objects.description: Every object created/updated in Phase 4, with its type, so the reviewer fetches each source.

type.description: Object type, e.g. PROG/P (program), PROG/I (include), DYNP (screen), CUAD (GUI status), FUGR, CLAS, TABL, DTEL, DOMA.

prog.description: Main program name (optional convenience field; also derivable from objects[]).

iteration.description: Review iteration number (1-3). A re-review after MAJOR fixes increments this (optional).

environment_context.description: Optional operating context for the reviewer — known backend outages and human-approved spec deviations — so a live infrastructure failure or an already-approved deviation is not miscounted as a code defect (see docs/DECISIONS.md D-013). Omit the whole object when there is nothing to report.

known_outages.description: Backend/tool outages in effect during this run, so the reviewer treats a resulting SKIPPED verification step as an environment gap rather than a finding.

items.description: One outage: what is down and which verification step it affects (fields are optional; fill in what is known).

component.description: What is down, e.g. 'ABAP Unit ADT execution (HTTP 404)' or 'ZMCP_ADT_SRV Textpool service (HTTP 500)'.

affected_step.description: Which verification.json / checklist step this affects and how, e.g. 'unit_test — RunUnitTest SKIPPED, recorded SKIPPED in verification.json'.

approved_deviations.description: Spec deviations a human already approved, so the reviewer does not re-flag them as violations against spec.md's literal text.

items.description: One approved deviation, with enough of a paper trail for the reviewer to verify the approval is real (fields are optional; fill in what is known).

description.description: What deviates from spec.md, and where (object/section).

notes.description: Free-text environment context that doesn't fit known_outages / approved_deviations (optional).
